mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid acceleration in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending corporate revenue contraction, triggering risk-off positioning that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment acceleration signal.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising housing starts and falling residential mortgage debt indicates a structural shift in household balance sheets toward real assets rather than consumption, which historically precedes equity market corrections over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term (HOUST up, RSAFS down) shows no statistically significant predictive power for quarterly SPX returns (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and robust aggregate supply, which supports corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth decouples from equity valuations, resulting in a negative or zero correlation between INDPRO month-over-month changes and 20-day SPX returns.

mechanism_narrative: Continuous quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP expands the nominal earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, providing fundamental support that drives positive forward equity returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: Real GDP growth fails to translate into earnings per share growth, causing the forward return correlation to drop below 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 5.0%, borrowing costs exceed the historical average corporate return on invested capital, compressing equity risk premiums and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Equity valuations remain resilient or expand despite funds rates exceeding 5.0%, demonstrating that the cost-of-capital channel is fully offset by strong earnings growth.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong nonfarm payroll growth and a declining unemployment rate indicate a tight labor market that sustains wage inflation, forcing the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy longer than markets expect, which ultimately pressures equity multiples.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE consistently coincides with positive SPX forward returns, indicating that labor strength is interpreted as a growth premium rather than an inflation risk.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the consumer price index signals successful disinflation, which reduces uncertainty and allows real disposable income to recover, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: CPI declines trigger recession fears that dominate the disinflation benefit, resulting in negative forward equity returns for > 50% of observations.
